The Company provides asset management services
across various schemes of UTI Mutual Fund, including
both active & passive options, along with providing
portfolio management services, advisory services, POP services, & other services.

UTI AMC is one of India’s leading Asset Management Companies, serving over 1.33 crore investor folios across diverse customer segments. From first-time investors and young professionals to middle-income families, NRIs, and global investors, the Company offers tailored investment solutions that address varied financial needs. With a strong focus on long-term customer value, UTI AMC continues to expand its reach and presence across India, delivering products aligned with the evolving goals of its investor base.
Additionally, it offers discretionary Portfolio Management Services (PMS) to organisations such as the Employees’ Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) and the Coal Mines Provident Fund Organisation. The Company also provides Non-Discretionary PMS to Postal Life Insurance (PLI).

Compliance is identified as a risk due to the frequent changes in regulatory requirements, making it challenging to consistently maintain adherence across all applicable laws. Operating in the financial sector, the Company manages large volumes of sensitive client information, and any lapses — whether in data protection, operational processes, or reporting — could result in regulatory breaches, penalties, and reputational harm.

The Company undertakes regular reviews of applicable compliance requirements and actively monitors regulatory updates to ensure the timely implementation of necessary changes. We have a software in place (“Velox”) wherein every employee has to submit the details of their investments/holdings and dealings, etc., including prior approval for trading. The regulatory requirements, as given by SEBI, are built within the software. To further strengthen compliance management, periodic training is provided to employees, emphasising the importance of regulatory adherence and raising awareness of the consequences of non-compliance.

The Company’s reliance on digital infrastructure for managing transactions, customer data, and internal operations makes data protection and information security a critical area of risk. With the increasing frequency and sophistication of cyber threats—such as phishing, ransomware, and third-party breaches—the potential for data compromise poses significant operational, reputational, and financial consequences.
The enactment of regulations such as the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA), 2023 has further elevated the importance of responsible data governance, placing legal obligations on the Company to protect personal data and ensure privacy rights of individuals. Any failure to comply with DPDPA or effectively secure sensitive information could result in penalties, legal liabilities, and erosion of investor trust.
Protecting customer confidentiality, ensuring business continuity, and maintaining investor confidence are central to the Company’s long-term credibility.

To mitigate data protection and information security risks, the Company has established a comprehensive governance framework, anchored by its “Policy on Privacy and Information Security,” which defines the rights and obligations related to the use and protection of personally identifiable and confidential investor information.
Our organisation has implemented a robust cybersecurity management framework based on international standards such as ISO/IEC 27001 and NIST Cybersecurity Framework, and in line with SEBI circular related to Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework. A dedicated Data Leak Policy, regular Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing (VAPT), Red Teaming, and half yearly Cyber Audit further strengthen the Company’s defences.
We have implemented several initiatives: Data Leak Protection solution, Continuous Automated Red Teaming , Breach and Attack Simulation and Attack Surface Monitoring solution, Endpoint Detection and Response solutions, Cyber Threat Intelligence (CTI) - Brand, Domain and Dark Web Monitoring , and Software Bill of Materials (SBOM). In parallel, employee awareness is actively enhanced through quarterly phishing simulations and regular communication campaigns to address evolving cyber threats. These measures collectively ensure proactive risk management, compliance with regulatory frameworks and protection of stakeholders’ interests.

Transparent operations and comprehensive, timely, and accurate disclosures present a strategic opportunity to strengthen stakeholder confidence and reinforce the Company’s reputation for integrity. By providing clear and reliable financial and non-financial information, the Company fosters trust among clients, investors, and regulators.
Beyond enhancing brand credibility, adherence to transparency requirements ensures regulatory compliance, helping to avoid penalties and legal risks. Furthermore, transparent reporting can help identify inefficiencies and areas for improvement within the Company.

While transparency and disclosures are an opportunity, the Company recognises the potential risks associated with inaccurate, delayed, or incomplete reporting. To mitigate these, the Company has established strong internal controls, a clear disclosure policy, and regular oversight mechanisms to ensure the accuracy, timeliness, and completeness of all disclosures. Regular meetings are held with stakeholders to discuss strategies or concerns, if any. The Company ensures all marketing materials accurately reflect the performance, risks, and benefits of investment products.

Business ethics and corporate governance are critical to maintaining the trust of investors, regulators, clients, and other stakeholders. Any lapse in ethical conduct, transparency, or governance practices can lead to reputational damage, regulatory scrutiny, financial penalties, and loss of stakeholder confidence.
Given the highly regulated nature of the asset management industry, even perceived non-compliance or conflicts of interest can adversely impact the Company’s credibility and long-term performance. The evolving expectations of stakeholders regarding ethical behaviour, Board accountability, and transparency further heighten this risk.
Recognising business ethics and governance as a risk ensures that the Company remains vigilant, continuously strengthens its internal controls and oversight mechanisms, and upholds the highest standards of integrity and accountability across all levels of operation.

UTI AMC has instituted a strong governance framework backed by policies such as the Code of Conduct, Anti-Bribery policy, Whistleblower Policy, and Fiduciary Duty Policy, all applicable across the organisation. These are designed to uphold ethical standards, ensure compliance, and mitigate governance-related risks.
Regular training programmes are conducted for employees and directors to reinforce ethical conduct and keep them updated on regulatory developments and internal policies. Governance Committees and internal audits further strengthen oversight and accountability.

Stakeholder engagement offers a valuable opportunity to build trust, enhance transparency, and align the Company’s actions with stakeholder expectations. Engaging with partners ensures they are well-informed, enabling them to recommend appropriate products and reducing instances of mis-selling due to misinformation. This not only strengthens investor protection but also reinforces the Company’s reputation for ethical conduct. Proactive engagement supports better decision-making, fosters long-term relationships, and positions the Company as a responsible and responsive asset manager.

While stakeholder engagement is seen as a strategic opportunity, the Company acknowledges that ineffective or inconsistent engagement may lead to misunderstandings, misaligned expectations, or reputational risks. To mitigate this, the Company follows a structured engagement approach involving regular interactions, transparent communication, and feedback mechanisms tailored to each stakeholder group. To strengthen internal stakeholder communication, the Company has adopted an Employee Engagement & Communication Policy, available on its website, which reflects its commitment to fostering a motivated and informed workforce.
Engagement with distribution partners is further supported through product training and knowledge-sharing sessions to ensure accurate information dissemination and reduce the risk of mis-selling. These initiatives help create a transparent, responsive, and inclusive engagement environment that supports long-term stakeholder trust and alignment with organisational goals.

Identifying risk management as an opportunity allows the Company to proactively strengthen its operational resilience, regulatory compliance, and stakeholder confidence. A robust risk management framework enables early identification and mitigation of potential threats, minimising disruptions and enhancing decision-making across functions. By embedding risk awareness into strategic planning and day-to-day operations, the Company can respond more effectively to market volatility, regulatory changes, and emerging risks. Integrating ESG considerations into risk assessments reflects the Company’s proactive approach to emerging trends and stakeholder expectations.

Although risk management is classified as an opportunity, the Company recognises that ineffective or outdated risk practices can expose it to operational, financial, reputational, and regulatory risks. To mitigate these, the Company has developed a comprehensive and dynamic Risk Management Framework. The framework includes structured risk identification, assessment, and control mechanisms across strategic, operational, investment, and ESG domains. It is regularly reviewed and updated to ensure that the Company remains agile, resilient, and prepared to adapt in a rapidly changing environment.

By prioritising customer protection and satisfaction, the Company can differentiate itself and gain a competitive advantage in a highly competitive market. This strategic focus can drive business growth by expanding market share and strengthening the Company’s brand reputation. Building strong customer relationships through exceptional service, robust grievance redressal mechanisms, and effective protection measures enhances client trust, retention, and loyalty. Satisfied customers are more likely to continue investing, increase their engagement, and refer the Company to others—contributing to long-term growth and sustained success.

While customer protection and satisfaction are viewed as strategic opportunities, the Company recognises that failure to meet customer expectations or lapses in protection mechanisms can pose reputational and regulatory risks. To mitigate such risks, the Company maintains a proactive customer service strategy, supported by clearly defined grievance redressal processes and periodic reviews of customer feedback. Additionally, the Company has established a comprehensive risk management approach, including strong compliance frameworks, regular internal audits, and strict adherence to regulatory standards. Transparent and accurate information disclosure, supported by robust internal controls, reinforces client trust and operational integrity.

Identifying human capital development as a strategic opportunity enables the Company to build a skilled, future-ready workforce aligned with evolving business needs. Targeted investments in employee training, upskilling, and educational programs not only improve individual and organisational performance but also enhance our competitive edge in attracting and retaining top talent within the asset management industry. These efforts foster a culture of continuous learning, innovation, and adaptability—key drivers of long-term resilience and value creation. Moreover, a strong focus on human capital development supports employee engagement and career growth, contributing to a more motivated and productive workforce.

As human capital development is considered an opportunity, no specific mitigation measures are required. However, the Company proactively invests in employee training, with a focus on regulatory awareness, ethical conduct, and compliance best practices. A strong organisational culture—rooted in shared values, professionalism, and a commitment to excellence—supports these efforts. Structured upskilling and development programmes have been implemented to equip employees with the capabilities needed to thrive in a dynamic financial environment. These initiatives emphasise enhancing technical expertise, leadership skills, digital fluency, and behavioural competencies to drive sustained business performance.

The Company actively integrates relevant acts, rules and regulations, and recommendations into the development of its policies and procedures. Our Business Responsibility & Sustainability Reporting Policy aligns with key national and international frameworks, including the National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct (NGRBC), the UN Guiding Principles for Business and Human Rights (UNGPs), the UN Sustainable
Development Goals (SDGs), the Paris Agreement on Climate Change, the Core Conventions of the International Labor Organisation (ILO), and the Indian Companies Act 2013.
Furthermore, UTI is dedicated to initiatives such as Climate Action 100+ and the United Nations Principles for Responsible Investment (UNPRI), reinforcing our dedication to sustainability and responsible business practices. In our ESG disclosures, we adhere to globally recognised reporting frameworks, including the IIRC framework, GRI Standards 2021, and SASB Standards. These standards thoroughly address material ESG factors included in both our annual and sustainability reports.

Aspiring to be the preferred wealth manager, the Company prioritises sustainability across all facets of its operations. With a steadfast focus on making a positive impact and fostering inclusive, broad-based growth for all stakeholders, the Company undertakes several initiatives:
Empowering Communities: Through our CSR initiatives, we aim to enhance education, healthcare, and rural development, continually prioritising community empowerment.
Environmental Responsibility: By investing in renewable energy at our corporate tower, reducing single-use plastics, and implementing responsible waste management practices, the Company actively works to reduce, minimise, and monitor its carbon footprint.
Promoting DE&I: The Company fosters a culture of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DE&I) in the workplace, alongside merit-based hiring. We support workforce upskilling to ensure employees' overall growth and adaptability to the evolving business landscape.
Employee Well-being: Demonstrating our commitment to the health of our employees, we have launched numerous programs aimed at enhancing their physical and emotional well-being.
Human Capital Development: The Company is dedicated to the continuous development of its employees by offering training and development programs that enhance skills, knowledge, and career growth opportunities for all employees.

Promoting DE&I: The Company fosters a culture of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DE&I) in the workplace, alongside merit-based hiring. We support workforce upskilling to ensure employees' overall growth and adaptability to the evolving business landscape.
Employee Well-being: Demonstrating our commitment to the health of our employees, we have launched numerous programs aimed at enhancing their physical and emotional well-being.
Human Capital Development: The Company is dedicated to the continuous development of its employees by offering training and development programs that enhance skills, knowledge, and career growth opportunities for all employees.

Aspiring to be the preferred wealth manager, the Company prioritises sustainability across all facets of its operations. With a steadfast focus on making a positive impact and fostering inclusive, broad-based growth for all stakeholders, the Company undertakes several initiatives:
Empowering Communities: Through our CSR initiatives, we aim to enhance education, healthcare, and rural development, continually prioritising community empowerment.
Environmental Responsibility: By investing in renewable energy at our corporate tower, reducing single-use plastics, and implementing responsible waste management practices, the Company actively works to reduce, minimise, and monitor its carbon footprint.
Promoting DE&I: The Company fosters a culture of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DE&I) in the workplace, alongside merit-based hiring. We support workforce upskilling to ensure employees' overall growth and adaptability to the evolving business landscape.
Employee Well-being: Demonstrating our commitment to the health of our employees, we have launched numerous programs aimed at enhancing their physical and emotional well-being.
Human Capital Development: The Company is dedicated to the continuous development of its employees by offering training and development programs that enhance skills, knowledge, and career growth opportunities for all employees.

Spreading Financial Literacy
1. Conducted over 890 Investor Awareness Programs (IAPs), reaching 72,769 prospective investors, including 291 IAPs specifically for 12,731 women.
2. Project FLY (Financial Literacy for Youth): Launched in March 2025, the pilot covered 7 colleges in Mumbai and Navi Mumbai through classroom IAPs, online quizzes, and SEBI’s Certification for Investor Program. The initiative engaged 764 students in classroom IAPs, with 568 students registering for SEBI’s Certification Program, of which 462 students (82%) passed the exam.

Community Development through CSR Initiatives
In the reporting year, our community development programs positively impacted 25,072 beneficiaries, encompassing initiatives in education, healthcare access and elderly care.
1. Educational Support: Over 24,871 underprivileged children were supported through targeted educational programs.This includes 265 differently abled children, who received specialized support and inclusive learning opportunities tailored to their needs.
2. Cancer cure: In FY 2024–25, the Company supported the treatment of 34 underprivileged cancer patients by providing financial assistance through the Indian Cancer Society’s Cancer Cure Fund.

Reducing Our Environmental Impact
1. Energy Audit: In FY 2024–25, an energy audit was conducted by the Industry Energy Assessment Cell at IIT Bombay. Implementation of the audit’s recommendations is currently in progress. Based on internal feasibility and priority, the Company has initiated implementation of select measures - Power Factor (PF) correction at UTI Tower and AHU belt replacements.
2. Energy Efficiency: Improvements include upgraded LED lighting, enhanced thermal insulation, and smart office monitoring systems to reduce energy use. To strengthen our initiatives, we partnered with Atsuya Technologies in October 2024 to implement HVAC and electricity monitoring at the Mumbai Corporate Office and the Chennai Branch Office/UFC. This collaboration provides access to a real-time dashboard for indoor temperature monitoring, enabling us to optimise temperature settings and achieve greater energy savings.
3. Renewable Energy: The Corporate Office operates entirely on renewable energy, backed by Green Energy Certificates, underscoring the commitment to reducing the carbon footprint.
4. Paperless Transition: Our digital Document Management System
(UTI E-Way) continues to evolve annually, supporting our goal of becoming a paperless office.
5. Waste Management: At the Corporate Office, all wet waste is composted, while paper and plastic waste are fully recycled through a waste management agency.A recycling policy ensures that old computers and laptops are responsibly disposed of through government authorised vendors.

Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI)
1. Women represent 26.49% of our permanent workforce.
2. Women held 12.30% of mid-senior to leadership positions within our permanent workforce.
3. Session on Inclusive Behaviour toward Persons with Disabilities (PwDs): Designed to raise awareness, challenge stereotypes, and equip employees with practical tools to engage respectfully and effectively with differently abled individuals, the session focused on inclusive workplace practices.
4. Training on Unconscious Bias: These sessions helped employees recognise and address hidden biases that can influence decision-making, hiring, team dynamics, and performance evaluations.

At UTI AMC, we regard Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) principles
as core to our business strategy and long-term value creation. ESG is not a parallel initiative, but a lens through which we evaluate risks, unlock opportunities, and engage with stakeholders. Our efforts are rooted in the belief that integrating ESG considerations enhances organisational resilience, supports sustainable outcomes, and strengthens stakeholder trust.
A key focus has been the deepening of ESG considerations into our investment practices while maintaining alignment with our fiduciary responsibilities. Simultaneously, we have responded to the dynamic and expanding regulatory landscape, which demands both greater transparency and rigor in disclosures. The systematic assessment and integration of material ESG risks into our risk management framework and mitigation strategies has been an essential step in strengthening future readiness and embedding ESG within core risk governance processes. In parallel, driving consistency in ESG adoption across internal functions and value chain remains a complex but necessary undertaking.
We have achieved meaningful progress across environmental, social, and governance dimensions. On the environmental front, we continued to strengthen our operational sustainability. Our Corporate Office is 100% powered by renewable energy, supported by Green Energy Certificates. Our waste management initiatives now ensure 100% composting of wet waste, full recycling of paper and plastic at the corporate tower, and responsible e-waste disposal via authorised vendors across locations. Our digital transformation continues with the ongoing evolution of UTI E-Way, our internal document management system that supports our paperless transition.
In the social domain, we scaled our financial literacy outreach significantly. In FY 2024–25, we conducted over 890 Investor Awareness Programs (IAPs), reaching 72,769 prospective investors—including nearly 13,000 women through dedicated sessions. The launch of Project FLY (Financial Literacy for Youth) marked a new milestone in our youth engagement strategy, with strong participation from college students and high success rates in SEBI certification. Our CSR efforts positively impacted over 25,000 individuals, with focused interventions in education, health, and elderly care—including specialised educational support for differently abled children and financial assistance for underprivileged cancer patients.
Within the organisation, we continued our work in advancing Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI). We expanded DEI-related training through sessions on unconscious bias and inclusive behaviours for working with persons with disabilities. Improvements in gender representation at hiring and leadership levels reflect our commitment to long-term change. Employee engagement around wellness and inclusion remains a key priority as we foster a safe, supportive, and equitable workplace.
Governance remains the anchor of our ESG commitment. ESG oversight is driven by the CSR and ESG Committee, and robust internal policies covering responsible investment, stewardship, and voting. Employee training on ESG principles continues, supporting consistent application across functions. Cybersecurity and data protection remain high-priority areas, supported by enhanced protocols and a Zero Trust approach to network security.

UTI AMC is committed to upholding the highest standards of ethics and integrity in its operations and responsibilities towards stakeholders. To support this, the Company has established robust, Board-approved policies covering Fiduciary Duty, Anti-Money Laundering (AML), Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption, Anti-Fraud, and guidelines on the giving and acceptance of gifts. These are accessible to employees via the Company’s intranet and website.
Integrity, honesty, trust, and quality are core values that guide UTI AMC’s interactions with investors and management of their investments. The Anti-Bribery Policy outlines clear guidelines to ensure fair, transparent business practices and mitigate legal and financial risks associated with bribery and corruption. This policy forms an integral part of the Company’s broader vigilance and compliance programmes.
Employees are expected to adhere to all policies and receive regular training to stay updated, particularly on anti-corruption procedures. Bribery, illegal payments, and corrupt practices are strictly prohibited, as they contradict the Company’s ethical principles.
The Anti-Fraud Policy addresses both internal and external fraud, including third parties associated with the Company. The AML Policy is aligned with SEBI guidelines and the Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002, outlining mutual fund obligations.
The Company also enforces strict rules under its gift policy, prohibiting employees from offering gifts of any value to third parties, including those affiliated with regulatory bodies, stock exchanges, financial institutions, brokers, dealers, or financial media.
There have been no penalties/fines/actions taken by regulators during the financial year 2024-25.

e. By strictly adhering to these guidelines and obtaining the necessary approvals, the Company upholds its commitment to ethical conduct and effective governance, thereby avoiding conflicts of interest.

While UTI AMC’s line of business does not inherently involve any significant occupational health and safety (OHS) risks, the Company has proactively implemented a comprehensive OHS Management System to ensure a safe, supportive, and functional workplace for all employees. This system covers all areas within the Company’s control or influence that may affect employee well-being.
The Company’s approach is rooted in the belief that employee well-being—both physical and mental—is essential to fostering a secure work environment and enhancing productivity. Our health and safety management system is built on four key pillars:
Compliance: UTI AMC strictly adheres to statutory health and safety regulations. Physical security measures include annual evacuation drills, fire safety briefings, hands-on training in the use of firefighting equipment, routine inspections and refilling of fire extinguishers, and monthly checks of detectors and sensors.
Risk Management: The Company regularly identifies, assesses, and mitigates OHS-related hazards and risks to minimise potential harm.
Training: Employees receive comprehensive OHS training, including orientation, fire safety procedures, and on-the-job safety guidance to ensure preparedness and awareness.
Collaboration: UTI AMC promotes active participation and consultation with employees and other stakeholders in setting and executing annual OHS objectives.
Additionally, UTI AMC partners with hospitals to provide annual health checkups and organises regular wellness webinars that address physical, mental, and emotional health.

Due to the nature of the business, work-related hazards are minimal. Nevertheless, the Company maintains a proactive approach to identifying and mitigating both routine and non-routine risks with employee well-being at the core. Processes followed include:
- Health Risk Assessments: Conducted in partnership with a healthcare service provider, these cover physical mobility, body pain impact, emotional well-being, fatigue, and energy levels, helping to identify potential occupational health concerns and areas of intervention.
- Routine Workplace Interventions: Regular walkthroughs and visual assessments are conducted to monitor equipment, work practices, and environmental conditions for any emerging risks.
- Non-Routine Risk Monitoring: E-Surveillance and ad-hoc audits are carried out to identify less frequent but critical hazards such as fire, electrical faults, or security breaches.
- Emergency Preparedness: Regular fire drills, safety advisories, and emergency response training ensure readiness. Security personnel are trained in fire safety protocols and equipment use.
- Employee Awareness: Safety-related advisories are shared periodically to enhance risk awareness and promote a culture of safety.
While complete elimination of risk may not always be possible, UTI AMC remains committed to taking all reasonably practical measures to ensure a secure and healthy work environment for all employees.

The Company is committed to maintaining a safe and secure working environment through a robust compliance framework designed to protect individuals from potential workplace accidents or injuries. This commitment is embedded in our Employee Health and Safety Policy, which outlines measures to ensure employee well-being.
To uphold workplace safety, the Company implements a range of initiatives, including comprehensive risk assessments, enhancements to safety equipment, fire safety training and drills, routine safety inspections, and risk mitigation measures during UFC (branch) visits. Additionally, UTI AMC places a strong emphasis on both the physical and psychological well-being of its employees. Regular health checkups are conducted, and employees are provided with term life insurance, personal accident coverage, and medical insurance. New employees receive comprehensive health screenings upon joining. Ongoing preventive health check-ups are conducted biennially for employees under 40, and annually for those aged 40 and above.
These measures are reinforced through periodic quality assurance checks and health awareness initiatives, fostering a workplace culture that prioritises holistic well-being and safety.
Based on the internal assessment, no corrective action was taken and no significant risks/concerns arose during the FY.

At UTI AMC, stakeholders are recognised as individuals and groups that contribute to the organization’s long-term value creation in alignment with its core vision and mission. This diverse group includes employees at all levels, unit holders, banks, custodians, distributors, independent financial advisors, business associates,
shareholders, regulators, and the wider community. The Company has established a structured framework to identify and engage with key stakeholders across its value chain.
UTI AMC acknowledges that each stakeholder group has distinct priorities and expectations. Through regular and meaningful engagement, both formal and informal, the Company gathers valuable insights that inform its approach to risk management, strategy development, business planning, and execution. Engagement channels are customized to ensure relevance and effectiveness for each stakeholder group.
In FY 2023–24, the Company undertook a comprehensive Double Materiality Assessment, engaging key stakeholders to understand their perspectives on material ESG issues. This process ensures that UTI AMC’s sustainability initiatives remain aligned with both internal value creation and broader environmental and social impact.

At UTI Asset Management Company, stakeholder engagement is an integral part of our governance framework, especially on economic, environmental, and social topics. We have established structured mechanisms to consult with key stakeholder groups—including investors, distributors, employees, regulators, portfolio companies, and civil society—on material sustainability issues. These consultations are carried out through formal surveys, ESG-focused activities, such as materiality assessment, distributor training sessions, employee feedback platforms, and engagements with investee companies. Insights gathered from these interactions are systematically analysed and used to shape our ESG integration and overall business strategy.
The Board is kept informed of stakeholder concerns and priorities through periodic updates from the various Committees: Corporate Social Responsibility and ESG Committee, Risk Management Committee, Audit Committee, Nomination and Remuneration Committee, Stakeholders Relationship Committee, and Unitholder Protection Committee. These Committees synthesise stakeholder inputs and present them during Board meetings, particularly in the context of strategy reviews, materiality assessments, product design, and disclosures. In cases where stakeholder consultations are delegated to functional teams or external experts, the resulting feedback is channelled back to the Board through documented reports and dashboards.
This ensures that the Board’s decisions on economic performance, environmental stewardship, and social responsibility are grounded in stakeholder expectations and aligned with global standards such as SEBI BRSR, UN PRI, and the Paris Agreement.

The Company’s strategy focuses on embedding social responsibility into its core business functions by translating its ESG commitments into actionable policies. To enhance its services, the Company conducts root cause analysis of customer complaints, grievances and suggestions, driving continuous improvement.
ESG focus areas are identified through a comprehensive materiality assessment, conducted in consultation with both internal and external stakeholders. Engaging with consultants and industry experts further deepens the understanding of stakeholder expectations. The key material issues identified are prioritised and integrated into the development of the ESG roadmap, which guides both strategic and operational decisions aimed at fostering climate resilience and sustainability into UTI’s business operations.
Ongoing feedback from stakeholders is consistently incorporated into the Company’s policies and practices, ensuring alignment with their needs and expectations. Regular interactions with investors and analysts provide valuable insights, which are reflected in the Company’s ESG framework. This leads to the implementation of initiatives focused on responsible investing, equal opportunity, diversity and inclusion, privacy, human capital, environmental impact, and the identification and management of sustainability-related risks.

Respect for human rights is a core value of the Company, guiding its commitment to fair, ethical, and inclusive business and employment practices. The Company strives to support, protect, and promote human rights across its operations, ensuring a workplace that upholds dignity, respect, and equal opportunities for all.
To reinforce this commitment, the Company has a zero-tolerance policy against all forms of child labour, forced labour, slavery, human trafficking, physical, sexual, psychological, or verbal abuse.
The Company has established robust mechanisms to uphold and safeguard human rights, including:
- POSH Policy for preventing and addressing sexual harassment in the workplace;
- Whistleblower Policy to encourage the reporting of any ethical violations;
- Grievance Redressal Mechanism to resolve employee concerns through a structured process;
- Equal Opportunity Policy to ensure fairness in recruitment, development, and promotion; and
- Code of conduct outlining standards for ethical and professional behaviour.
These policies are regularly communicated and made accessible via the Company’s intranet and reinforced through employee training and awareness initiatives.
The Company regularly reviews employment practices, benefits, facilities, and services to eliminate any unlawful discrimination and ensure equitable treatment. It maintains an inclusive, open-door policy, fostering a collaborative and transparent work environment where concerns are typically resolved at this supervisory level. To proactively address employee concerns the Company engages in regular interactions and conducts comprehensive exit interviews, continuously refining workplace practices.
Additionally, employees and stakeholders are encouraged to report any human rights violations through the Internal Committee, Whistleblower Mechanism, and the Grievance Redressal Processes outlined in the policies available publicly and on the Company’s intranet.

The Company is committed to upholding a safe, respectful, and harmonious work environment where all individuals are treated with dignity and fairness, regardless of ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, race, caste, gender, disability, job designation, or any other protected characteristic. By fostering an inclusive and equitable culture, the Company actively works to ensure that workplace practices reflect principles of diversity, inclusion, and fairness.
As a part of its dedication to supporting, protecting, and promoting human rights, the Company has established robust policies and Committees to address grievances and complaints related to workplace conduct and ethical business practices. These include:
- Internal Committee under the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition, and Redressal) Act, 2013 – ensuring a harassment free workplace.
- Whistleblower Policy - encouraging the reporting of ethical violations and workplace concerns without fear of retaliation.
To reinforce these commitments, the Company:
- Ensures policy details, Committee members, and legal consequences of violations are prominently displayed across all offices and UFCs (branch offices).
- Conducts regular awareness sessions to educate employees about workplace policies, reporting mechanisms, and the prevention of sexual harassment.
- Provides easy access to policy documents through the Company’s intranet, ensuring employees and stakeholders are informed of their rights and the available grievance mechanism.
The Whistleblower Policy serves as a safeguard against discrimination, harassment, victimisation, or unfair treatment, including:
- Retaliation, intimidation, or threats against employees who report violations;
- Unjust disciplinary actions, suspension, or biased behaviour towards whistleblowers; and
- Obstruction of duties or responsibilities for individuals making protected disclosures
The Company takes proactive measures to minimise any difficulties whistleblowers may face, ensuring that they can perform their responsibilities without fear of disadvantage or retaliation. All employees and stakeholders are encouraged to report any unethical practices or human rights violations, reinforcing the Company’s commitment to an inclusive, ethical, and responsible workplace.
Based on the internal assessment, no corrective actions were taken or significant risks/concerns arose during the FY.

The Company remains committed to upholding human rights by actively monitoring and adapting its internal processes. One such initiative has been the integration of key policies such as the Code of Conduct, Whistleblower Policy, Anti-Money Laundering Policy, POSH Policy, and Cybersecurity policy, into its learning and development framework.
To reinforce awareness and sensitivity, employees are mandated to undergo periodic training and complete an annual certification process, ensuring continuous education and compliance. This structured approach helps prevent human rights violations and promotes a responsible workplace culture.
The Company has not conducted a formal human rights due diligence exercise. However, internal assessments on certain topics have been carried out.

As part of its commitment to minimising greenhouse gas emissions and improving energy efficiency, the
Company has undertaken a range of initiatives across its corporate and branch offices. These efforts aim to reduce environmental impact while enhancing operational performance.
a. Energy Efficiency: All offices have been upgraded with energy-efficient LED lighting, low-power monitors, improved insulation, and optimised heating systems. Office layouts maximise natural lighting, and digital learning and video conferencing have reduced the need for physical travel, thereby cutting energy use and emissions.
b. Energy audit: In FY 2024–25, an energy audit was conducted by the Industry Energy Assessment Cell at IIT Bombay. The audit presented several recommendations aimed at improving energy efficiency. Based on internal feasibility and priority, the Company has initiated implementation of select measures - Power Factor (PF) correction at UTI Tower and AHU belt replacements.
c. Smart Office Design: The Company has adopted smart technologies such as motion-sensor lighting and automated HVAC systems. Green-certified roller blinds (3% openness factor) have been installed
to minimize solar heat gain, contributing to more consistent and efficient temperature regulation.
d. Energy Friendly Air Conditioning Systems: Our air-conditioning systems use R-134A refrigerant
gas, compliant with the Energy Conservation Building Code (ECBC). The 2x300 TR chiller plants are
equipped with VFD compressors that optimise energy usage, especially during low-occupancy periods or
off-peak hours.
e. EV Charging: EV charging stations have been installed at the Corporate Office to support employees
adopting low-emission transport options.
f. Renewable Energy: The Corporate Office operates entirely on renewable energy, as certified by Tata Power, underlining our commitment to sustainable power solutions.

Given the nature of its business operations, the Company has limited scope for influence on these parameters.
However, the Company remains committed to addressing waste management in an environmentally responsible
manner. To this end, the Company actively promotes the reuse and recycling of waste generated in its business
operations, ensuring that all feasible opportunities forsustainable waste management are maximised. As
provided above, our operations lead to the generation of non-hazardous waste (dry waste, including plastic, paper, and wet waste) along with some e-waste.
The Company has engaged a specialised waste management agency to facilitate the collection, composting, and recycling of wet waste and dry waste (paper and plastic waste) from its corporate tower. For the responsible disposal of e-waste, including computers, printers, monitors, switches, modems, and scanners, the
Company adheres to its IT Asset Management Policy, which is accessible on its intranet and outlines the
prescribed processes for disposing of information assets.
To ensure regulatory compliance and adherence to policy guidelines, UTI AMC collaborates with governmentauthorised third-party vendors for IT asset disposal. recycling certificates are obtained from the vendor to verify proper disposal and recycling processes. Air conditioners and inverter batteries are sold to authorised dealers.

UTI AMC maintains a Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Policy, approved by its Board and accessible
on the Company’s intranet. The policy outlines clear operational procedures for responding to, recovering
from, and resuming operations at a predefined level following any disruption. In alignment with the objectives
outlined in the policy document, the Company’s business continuity and disaster recovery strategies are developed, implemented, tested, maintained, and operated to support all critical service lines and processes.
In case the primary working site, such as the UTI Tower, becomes partially or fully unavailable, alternate
arrangements and working procedures have been established. Additionally, in cases of applications outage
at the UTI data centre, a disaster recovery site is available to guarantee uninterrupted access to critical applications via the Internet.

In FY 2024–25, an energy audit was conducted by the Industry Energy Assessment Cell at IIT Bombay.
The audit presented several recommendations aimed at improving energy efficiency. Based on internal
feasibility and priority, the Company has initiated implementation of select measures - Power Factor (PF) correction at UTI Tower and AHU belt replacements.
Power Factor correction at UTI Tower resulted in energy savings of 218 kWh, while the replacement of AHU belts contributed to an additional 1,043 kWh in savings.

To strengthen our energy efficiency initiatives, we partnered with Atsuya Technologies in October 2024
to implement HVAC and electricity monitoring at the Mumbai Corporate Office and the Chennai UFC (Branch Office). This collaboration provides access to a real-time dashboard for indoor temperature monitoring, enabling us to optimise temperature settings and achieve greater energy savings.

Following the installation of the energy
monitoring system, UTI Tower achieved energy savings of 99,335 kWh. Similarly, the Chennai Main Branch recorded savings of 2,838 kWh after implementing energy monitoring and control modules.

The Company, through its initiative launched in 2009, known as ‘UTI Swatantra’, aims at empowering
individuals with the knowledge to achieve their financial goals through wealth creation. This initiative focuses on
educating the public about the benefits of mutual funds and their suitability for various types of investors. The
Company’s approach includes targeted communication strategies for distinct investment categories. By delivering
tailored content on the characteristics and suitability of each category, the Company ensures that investors are
well-informed when making investment choices. These communications are disseminated across a wide array
of channels, including paid media, social media, email, SMS, and WhatsApp, in multiple formats such as television
and print advertisements, audiovisual materials (AVs), GIFs, infographics, listicles, and articles.
Additionally, UTI AMC prioritises complete transparency regarding its offerings and adheres to all disclosure
requirements for its products and services, in line with AMFI and SEBI guidelines on product labelling within
risk and disclosure categories. Monthly disclosures of Riskometers, detailing scheme-wise risks, are provided
and made available on the Company’s website. The Riskometer is promptly updated whenever necessary,
with an addendum issued accordingly. Product literature containing Riskometer information for various schemes
is also maintained, ensuring that investors are clearly informed about the risk grade of each product.

The Company is dedicated to upholding a strong communication framework that ensures clear and timely
updates regarding any potential disruptions or service interruptions, thereby promoting transparency, reliability,
and trust in our operations. To support this commitment, the Company has implemented a comprehensive Disaster Recovery (DR) and Business Continuity Plan (BCP) that addresses all aspects of its business functions.
In the event of a service disruption, the Company utilises a variety of communication channels, both digital and offline, based on the severity of the issue. Key communication methods include letters, branch network, updates, SMS, emails, websites, mobile applications, and social media platforms. Additionally, we ensure that our contact centre agents are promptly informed about both planned and unplanned outages, enabling them to efficiently respond to customer inquiries. Customers are also proactively notified with estimated resolution times and provided with information on alternative service channels.

The Company places a strong emphasis on transparency with its customers, ensuring that all relevant information is communicated effectively through multiple channels, including emails, the website, and mobile applications. It prepares comprehensive Scheme Information Documents (SID) and Key Information Memorandums (KIM) for every mutual fund scheme it launches. Furthermore, the Company fully complies with all disclosure requirements mandated by SEBI and AMFI. To enhance accessibility, factsheets and product literature are also made available on the Company’s website.
The Company actively seeks customer feedback at various touchpoints. All customer complaints and grievances
are subjected to root cause analysis to drive service quality improvements. Through its contact centre, the
Company conducts Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) surveys following each voice and non-voice investor interaction
and monitors the Net Promoter Score (NPS) to evaluate overall satisfaction. In FY 2024–25, the average NPS
recorded was 96%. Customers who provide low ratings are proactively reached out to for additional feedback
and suggestions for improvement. A dedicated form for submitting feedback or suggestions is available on the
Company’s website at https://www.utimf.com/help-andsupport/write-to-us.
